QF.id: Introduction to AML, CFT and Proliferation Financing_9

question.text: Which of the following is the core legislation forming the legal framework put in place by India to combat money laundering?

answer.text: Securities and Exchange Board of India Act, 1992

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** The Prevention of Money Laundering Act, 2002 (PMLA) forms the core of the legal framework put in place by India to combat money laundering. Its objective is to prevent money laundering and provide for confiscation of property derived from or involved in money laundering.
**The Trap:** FEMA deals with foreign exchange transactions, UAPA deals with unlawful/terrorist activities, and SEBI Act governs securities markets. While all support AML efforts, PMLA is the dedicated, core AML law.
**Cross-Exam Context:** PMLA is the single most important legislation for the NISM XXIV exam. Every chapter references it. In NISM III-A and III-C, PMLA obligations of intermediaries are also tested.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** PMLA came into force on July 1, 2005 — not 2002 (which is when it was enacted). This distinction between enactment and enforcement is a favorite exam trick.
